latest.title: Dragonflies don't use their legs like we think
latest.script_key: watch those back legs they re not for walking dragonflies use them like a net to scoop prey out of the air in mid flight that s why they never stop moving they re always hunting did you think they just rested on reeds

latest.title: Dogs read human pointing surprisingly well
latest.script_key: dogs can follow a human point fast watch the eyes check the person because dogs are unusually good at using human gestures that skill helps them cooperate with us before any command gets repeated the small glance is social intelligence not random obedience what dog skill should come next

latest.title: Fossils turn old bones into time clues
latest.script_key: fossils can preserve a clue from deep time watch the shape in the rock because fossils form when buried remains leave mineral records behind the original body is gone but the pattern can reveal age habitat and behavior which ancient clue should we read next

latest.title: Coral reefs are cities built by tiny animals
latest.script_key: coral reefs are built by tiny living builders watch the reef texture because coral animals build hard skeletons that stack into habitat fish algae and countless small creatures then use that structure like a city the color sits on top of engineering which habitat next

latest.title: Glaciers carve valleys in real time
latest.script_key: this lake didn t start here that s why glaciers dragged rocks like sandpaper that scraped a basin a mile deep notice the smoothed cliffs the ice did that now the water fills the scratch marks did you learn in school that glaciers move slow this one moved faster than you think

latest.title: bats don't rely on sight at night
latest.script_key: watch this bat chase see those tiny ears twitch like radar dishes it s not seeing the moth it s hearing the flutter that s why the myth about bats being blind is half true they see fine but their ears do the heavy lifting would you trust your hearing over your eyes in the dark

latest.title: Rivers carve bends by stealing from one bank
latest.script_key: rivers move sideways while they flow watch the outside of the bend because faster water cuts that bank while slower water drops sediment inside the curve over time the whole river shifts across the landscape a bend is motion written in mud which water shape next now the river bend at the start makes sense

latest.title: Snow makes mountains younger not older
latest.script_key: here s the part nobody tells you every time a glacier scrapes downhill it carves away the sides but pushes rock upward in the middle that s why peaks stay sharp look at these layers glaciers aren t wearing mountains down they re giving them a facelift would you trust a bulldozer that builds as it destroys

latest.title: Climbers on Cotopaxi don’t stand on the summit
latest.script_key: watch this one twice those tiny dots on cotopaxi aren t climbers standing on the very top they re on a flat patch 20 meters below the true summit ridge because the ice is too thin and sloped to stand on that s why rescue teams mark the safe zone with orange poles
